title: Latent Upper Reasoner
research_question: Can the upper stack preserve global reasoning quality while operating over a compact latent sequence instead of the full token stream?
implementation_notes: Implemented via LATENT_REASONER_* env knobs that replace selected upper attention blocks with a pooled latent sequence, causal latent attention, and shifted token writeback.
The learned mode adds slot embeddings; the pooled mode uses pure sequence-derived latent slots.
This is a causality-safe first pass at latent upper reasoning rather than a full Perceiver-style cross-attention stack.

candidates.name: ad1-top2-learned-latent-reasoner
candidates.goal: Test whether the final two reasoning layers can operate on a compact latent sequence.
candidates.hypothesis: A learned-slot latent workspace may preserve most top-of-stack global reasoning while reducing token-space burden.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether latent reasoning is viable as a top-of-stack replacement.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the most direct first test of a latent upper reasoner without rewriting the whole upper stack.
candidates.description: replace the final two attention layers with a learned-slot latent upper reasoner

candidates.name: ad2-full-upper-learned-latent-reasoner
candidates.goal: Test whether the entire upper reasoning band wants to operate in a compact latent space.
candidates.hypothesis: The upper stack may be overpaying for full token-space reasoning and prefer a learned latent sequence instead.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether latent reasoning should be top-only or a full upper-stage design.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the bold version of the same question and attacks upper-stack memory/compute waste directly.
candidates.description: replace the full upper attention stack with a learned-slot latent upper reasoner

candidates.name: ad3-top2-pooled-summary-bank
candidates.goal: Test whether sequence-derived latent summaries work better than fixed learned slots.
candidates.hypothesis: A pooled summary bank may retain sequence structure better than learned latent slots at this scale.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the latent workspace should be learned and fixed or directly derived from the current sequence.
candidates.why_worthy: This separates the latent bottleneck idea from the learned-slot implementation choice.
candidates.description: use pooled latent summaries instead of learned slots for the final two reasoning layers

candidates.name: ad4-periodic-latent-reasoning
candidates.goal: Test whether latent reasoning works best as a periodic global consolidation step rather than a full replacement.
candidates.hypothesis: Interleaving normal upper attention with latent reasoning may preserve quality better than replacing the whole upper stage.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether latent reasoning is most useful as periodic consolidation.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the best bridge between the current `AA4` interleaving clue and a bolder latent upper-stack rethink.
candidates.description: interleave latent-reasoner blocks through the upper stack instead of replacing all upper attention

candidates.name: ad5-top3-learned-latent-reasoner
candidates.goal: Test a middle ground between top-only latent reasoning and full upper-stage replacement.
candidates.hypothesis: The upper stack may want a broader but still partial latent reasoning band rather than only the top two layers.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the latent reasoning sweet spot is top-only, full-upper, or an intermediate upper band.
candidates.why_worthy: This gives the latent branch a proper placement sweep instead of only two extremes.
candidates.description: replace the top three attention layers with a learned-slot latent upper reasoner
